title: Floating Shelf with Plywood Covering
summary: A hollow 1x3 shelf frame skinned in 1/4" plywood so no seams or fasteners show — a clean, modern floating shelf that hides its cleat.
description:
A modern floating shelf built as a hollow torsion-box: a frame of 1x3 on edge, wrapped in thin plywood so the finished shelf looks like a solid slab with no visible joinery or brackets. It mounts over a cleat screwed to the wall, and the plywood skin hides everything.

The frame is what carries the load, so build it well and screw it to studs. The internal ribs (the short 1x3 pieces) both stiffen the shelf and give the plywood skin something to land on — without them a long shelf skinned in 1/4" ply would feel hollow and flex. Space them along the length.

Skin the bottom first, then the front and side strips, cutting each to fit and attaching with 3/4" brad nails and glue. Cut-to-fit is the honest approach here because a hollow frame never comes out exactly nominal. Keep the fasteners minimal and set them where wood filler and a coat of paint erase them, and the shelf reads as one clean floating plane.

tools.note: Optional for the frame joints; brads and glue also work.
Ripping the plywood skin cleanly to width.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Build the basic 1x3 frame for the shelf — two long rails with the short ribs spaced along the length. The ribs stiffen the shelf and give the plywood skin something to fasten to. This frame is the structure, so make it solid; it's what gets screwed to the wall.

steps.title: Skin the bottom
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" plywood (or MDF) to the bottom of the frame with 3/4" brad nails and glue — 1" nails work too. Keep it aligned to the frame edges.

steps.title: Add the front strip
steps.body: Cut the plywood front strip to fit and attach it across the front of the shelf with 3/4" nails and glue, covering the frame edge.

steps.title: Add the side pieces and top
steps.body: Cut and attach the side plywood pieces, then the top skin, wrapping the frame completely. Cut each to fit — a hollow frame rarely finishes exactly nominal.

steps.title: Fill, finish, and hang
steps.body: Fill the few nail holes, sand, and finish. Screw a cleat to the wall studs and slide the shelf over it, fastening from below or behind so nothing shows on top. Confirm it's level before you set the final screws.

images.alt: Clean modern floating shelf skinned in plywood with no visible brackets
